name: Pipeline Integration
description: Wire all new leaf modules (padding.js, ttl.js, pseudonyms.js, discovery.js, crypto.js) into the existing codebase by modifying network.js, messages.js, and client.js to implement the full send and receive pipelines. Three targeted change sets applied in dependency order: (1) network.js — receive path restructuring with binary frame support, peer accessor exports, dynamic rebroadcast probability; (2) messages.js — async send path with stampTTL, pseudonym generation for channels, padding; (3) client.js — discovery bootstrap with dependency-injected network API. Channel messages skip signature verification (no pubKey). Peer exchange uses text frames, user messages use binary frames. Raw binary frames are rebroadcast as-is (never re-encoded).

types.name: MessageType
types.description: The type of message being sent through the pipeline. Determines pseudonym and signature behavior.

types.name: FromField

fields.name: name
fields.description: Display name or shortened pseudonym for channel messages.

fields.name: pubKey
fields.description: Base64-encoded public key. Present for direct and public messages, absent for channel (private) messages.

types.description: The sender identity attached to a message package. For channel (private) messages, pubKey is omitted and name is a shortened pseudonym. For DMs and public messages, pubKey is included.

types.name: MessagePackage

fields.name: type
fields.description: Message type: private, direct, or public.

fields.name: signature
fields.description: Base64-encoded signature over the message content.

fields.name: timestamp
fields.description: Unix timestamp in milliseconds via Date.now().

types.description: The full message object constructed by packageMessage, before serialization and padding. Includes TTL stamp after stampTTL is applied.

types.name: WebSocketFrame
types.description: A raw WebSocket frame as received by the onmessage handler. Text frames (string) carry protocol/discovery messages. Binary frames (ArrayBuffer) carry padded user messages.

types.name: NetworkAPI

fields.name: getConnectedPeerCount
fields.description: Function: () => int. Returns current number of connected peers.

fields.name: getConnectedPeerUrls
fields.description: Function: () => list[str]. Returns URLs of all currently connected peers.

fields.name: broadcastMessage
fields.description: Function: (frame: bytes|str) => None. Sends a frame to all connected peers.

fields.name: connectToPeer
fields.description: Function: (url: str) => None. Initiates a WebSocket connection to the given peer URL.

types.description: Dependency-injected network API object passed to initializeDiscovery. Provides accessor functions for peer state without exposing internal data structures.

types.name: PaddedFrame
types.description: A Uint8Array containing a padded message ready for binary WebSocket transmission. Produced by padMessage, consumed by broadcastMessage.

types.name: Configuration

fields.name: user
fields.description: Current user configuration including keys and display name.

types.description: Subset of the existing application configuration relevant to pipeline integration. Assumed to be available in scope of messages.js.

types.name: UserConfig

fields.name: privKey
fields.description: User's private/secret key (Uint8Array). NEVER log this value.

types.description: User identity configuration containing keys and display name.

types.name: ReceivePipelineResult
types.description: Outcome of processing a received frame through the receive pipeline.

functions.name: getConnectedPeerCount
functions.description: Returns the number of currently connected WebSocket peers. Pure accessor over the internal peers Map in network.js. Added as a new named export to network.js.
postconditions: Return value >= 0
Return value equals peers.size at time of call

functions.name: getConnectedPeerUrls
functions.description: Returns an array of URLs for all currently connected WebSocket peers. Pure accessor over the internal peers Map keys in network.js. Added as a new named export to network.js.
postconditions: Return value is a new array (not a reference to internal state)
Length of returned array equals getConnectedPeerCount()
Each element is a valid WebSocket URL string

functions.name: packageMessage
functions.description: Async function that builds, stamps, signs, serializes, and pads a message for broadcast. Modified from the existing synchronous packageMessage in messages.js. Preserves the existing function signature with new optional parameters. Steps: (1) build message object with existing logic, (2) stampTTL(messagePackage, type), (3) for channel messages: generate pseudonym from privKey+channelId, set from.name to shortenPseudonym(pseudonym), delete from.pubKey; for DM/public: keep existing from fields, (4) sign the message, (5) JSON.stringify, (6) padMessage(new TextEncoder().encode(json)) to produce PaddedFrame Uint8Array. Returns the padded binary frame ready for broadcast.

inputs.name: type
inputs.description: Message type determining pseudonym and signature behavior.

inputs.name: channelId
inputs.description: Channel identifier, required when type is 'private'. Used for pseudonym generation.

inputs.name: recipientPubKey
inputs.description: Recipient's public key for direct messages. Passed through to existing encryption logic.

error_cases.name: message_too_long
error_cases.condition: Serialized JSON exceeds maximum padded frame size (64KB)

error_cases.name: missing_channel_id
error_cases.condition: type is 'private' but channelId is null or undefined

preconditions: configuration.user.privKey is available in module scope
channelId must be provided when type is 'private'
postconditions: Returned bytes is a padded Uint8Array of length that is a power of 2
Returned bytes length <= 65536 (64KB max after padding)
The embedded message object has a ttl field set by stampTTL
For type='private': from.pubKey is absent in the serialized message
For type='private': from.name is a 16-character shortened pseudonym
For type='direct' or type='public': from.pubKey is present in the serialized message
The embedded message has a valid signature over message content

functions.name: handleMessageSubmit
functions.description: Async event handler for message submission from the UI. Modified from the existing synchronous handler in messages.js. Calls event.preventDefault() BEFORE any await to prevent form submission. Wraps packageMessage in try/catch to surface padding errors (message too long) as UI warnings via appendMessage. On success, passes the padded binary frame to broadcastMessage.

inputs.name: event
inputs.description: DOM Event object from form submission. preventDefault() is called synchronously before any async work.

error_cases.name: message_too_long_ui
error_cases.condition: packageMessage throws RangeError due to message exceeding 64KB after padding

error_cases.name: unexpected_send_error
error_cases.condition: Any non-RangeError exception during packageMessage or broadcast

preconditions: event is a valid DOM Event with preventDefault method
User has entered message content in the UI input field
Network is initialized and broadcastMessage is available
postconditions: event.preventDefault() was called synchronously (before any await)
On success: padded binary frame was passed to broadcastMessage
On success: UI input field is cleared
On padding error: UI displays 'Message too long (max 64KB)' warning via appendMessage
On other errors: error is logged via console.error
side_effects: Calls broadcastMessage to send padded frame to all peers
Updates DOM: clears input field on success or shows error message on failure

structured_side_effects.target: broadcastMessage — sends padded binary frame to all connected WebSocket peers
structured_side_effects.description: Broadcasts the padded message via WebSocket binary frames.

structured_side_effects.target: DOM — clears message input on success, shows error on failure
structured_side_effects.description: Updates UI elements to reflect send outcome.
Logs unexpected errors that are not padding-related.

functions.name: handleIncomingFrame
functions.description: The restructured WebSocket onmessage handler logic in network.js. Discriminates between text frames (protocol/discovery messages) and binary frames (padded user messages). For text frames: checks isProtocolMessage and routes to discovery handler. For binary frames: runs the full receive pipeline — unpad, deserialize, TTL check, dedup, signature verify, then passes to existing message handler callback. Uses try/catch around the entire binary processing path for malformed data resilience. Rebroadcasts valid messages using the original raw binary frame (never re-encoded) with dynamic probability from getRebroadcastProbability.

inputs.name: rawFrame
inputs.description: The raw event.data from the WebSocket onmessage event. Either a string (text frame) or ArrayBuffer (binary frame).

inputs.name: senderUrl
inputs.description: The WebSocket URL of the peer that sent this frame, used for logging and discovery routing.

error_cases.name: malformed_binary_frame
error_cases.condition: Binary frame cannot be unpadded, decoded as UTF-8, or parsed as JSON

error_cases.name: duplicate_message
error_cases.condition: Message ID already exists in deduplication set

error_cases.name: signature_verification_failed
error_cases.condition: Message has from.pubKey but verifySignature returns false

error_data.handling: console.warn('Signature verification failed for message:', message.id); return 'signature_failed_dropped'

error_cases.name: pubkey_without_signature
error_cases.condition: Message has from.pubKey but signature field is missing or empty

error_data.handling: console.warn('Message has pubKey but no signature:', message.id); return 'pubkey_without_signature_dropped'

preconditions: Deduplication set is available in module scope
postconditions: Text frames matching isProtocolMessage are routed to discovery handler and not processed further
Expired messages are dropped with console.warn and not forwarded
Duplicate messages are dropped silently and not forwarded
Messages with from.pubKey but invalid or missing signature are dropped with console.warn
Messages without from.pubKey (channel messages) skip signature verification entirely
Valid messages are passed to the message handler callback
Valid messages are rebroadcast with probability from getRebroadcastProbability using the ORIGINAL raw frame bytes
Malformed binary frames are caught, logged via console.error, and dropped
side_effects: May rebroadcast the original raw frame to connected peers
Calls message handler callback for valid messages
Routes protocol messages to discovery handler

structured_side_effects.target: WebSocket peers — rebroadcast of original raw frame
structured_side_effects.description: Forwards valid user messages to other peers using the unmodified binary frame.
Records the message ID to prevent processing duplicates.

structured_side_effects.target: console.warn for security drops (expired, bad signature); console.error for malformed data
structured_side_effects.description: Structured logging for pipeline drop reasons.

functions.name: broadcastMessage
functions.description: Modified version of the existing broadcastMessage in network.js to accept both string and Uint8Array/ArrayBuffer frames. For binary frames (Uint8Array or ArrayBuffer), sends via WebSocket as binary. For string frames (backward compatibility), sends as text. This preserves backward compatibility for any existing callers while enabling the new padded binary pipeline.

inputs.name: frame
inputs.description: The message frame to broadcast. Either a padded Uint8Array/ArrayBuffer (new pipeline) or a JSON string (legacy/backward compatibility).

error_cases.name: send_failure
error_cases.condition: WebSocket.send() throws for a specific peer (e.g., connection closing)

error_data.handling: Log error for that peer, continue sending to remaining peers

preconditions: At least one peer is connected (otherwise message is silently not sent)
frame is either a string, Uint8Array, or ArrayBuffer
postconditions: Frame is sent to all currently connected peers
Binary frames are sent as WebSocket binary frames
String frames are sent as WebSocket text frames
No encoding or transformation is applied to the frame
side_effects: Sends frame to all connected WebSocket peers

structured_side_effects.description: Sends the frame to every peer in the peers Map.

functions.name: initializeWebSocket
functions.description: Modification to the existing WebSocket creation logic in network.js. After creating the WebSocket instance, sets ws.binaryType = 'arraybuffer' to ensure binary frames arrive as ArrayBuffer (not Blob). This is a minimal targeted change to the existing connection setup code.

inputs.name: url
inputs.description: The WebSocket URL to connect to.

error_cases.name: connection_failed
error_cases.condition: WebSocket constructor throws or connection fails

error_data.handling: Existing error handling preserved — onerror and onclose callbacks handle cleanup

preconditions: url is a valid WebSocket URL (ws:// or wss://)
postconditions: Returned WebSocket has binaryType set to 'arraybuffer'
onmessage handler uses the restructured handleIncomingFrame logic
All existing WebSocket event handlers (onopen, onclose, onerror) are preserved
side_effects: Creates a new WebSocket connection to the specified URL

structured_side_effects.target: peers Map — adds new peer entry
structured_side_effects.description: Registers the new connection in the internal peers tracking.

functions.name: initializeDiscoveryBootstrap
functions.description: New initialization call added to client.js after initializeNetwork(). Constructs the NetworkAPI dependency object from network.js exports and passes it to initializeDiscovery from discovery.js. This wires up the discovery module without creating circular dependencies — discovery receives its network capabilities via dependency injection.

error_cases.name: network_not_initialized
error_cases.condition: Called before initializeNetwork() — peer accessors would return empty/undefined

error_data.handling: console.error('Cannot initialize discovery: network not ready')

preconditions: initializeNetwork() has already been called and completed
getConnectedPeerCount, getConnectedPeerUrls, broadcastMessage, and connectToPeer are available as imports from network.js
initializeDiscovery is available as import from discovery.js
postconditions: Discovery module is initialized with a valid NetworkAPI object
Discovery timers (peer exchange interval) are started
No circular module dependencies are created
side_effects: Starts discovery timers that will periodically exchange peer information

structured_side_effects.target: Discovery module internal state — timers and known peers cache
structured_side_effects.description: Initializes the discovery subsystem with network API references and starts periodic peer exchange.

invariants: Raw binary frames are rebroadcast as-is — the original bytes from the sender are forwarded without re-encoding, re-padding, or re-serialization
event.preventDefault() is always called synchronously in handleMessageSubmit BEFORE any await expression
Channel messages (type='private') never contain from.pubKey in the serialized package
Channel messages skip signature verification on receive — if from.pubKey is absent, verifySignature is not called
Messages with from.pubKey present but missing or invalid signature are dropped (security invariant)
All existing function signatures are preserved — new parameters are added as optional with defaults
No existing module is rewritten from scratch — only minimal, targeted additions and modifications
New imports are added at the top of each file alongside existing imports
Private keys, secret keys, and decrypted message content are NEVER logged
console.warn is used for security-relevant drops (expired TTL, failed signature); console.error is used only for actual errors (malformed data, missing dependencies)
Text frames are routed to the discovery/protocol handler; binary frames are routed to the user message pipeline — frame type discrimination happens first in onmessage
ws.binaryType is set to 'arraybuffer' for all WebSocket connections to ensure binary frames arrive as ArrayBuffer not Blob
The send pipeline order is: compose → encrypt → stampTTL → pseudonym (channels) → package → sign → serialize → pad → broadcast
The receive pipeline order is: receive → classify (protocol shortcut) → unpad → deserialize → TTL check → dedup → verify sig (if pubKey) → decrypt → display
Padding errors (message too long) in the send path are surfaced to the user via UI feedback, not silently swallowed
broadcastMessage accepts both string (backward compat) and Uint8Array/ArrayBuffer (new pipeline) without breaking existing callers
Discovery is bootstrapped via dependency injection of the NetworkAPI object, avoiding circular imports between network.js and discovery.js
